commit.message:
Add eth/69 protocol support to reach latest Geth nodes

Most mainnet peers (67%) were silently dropping us after Hello because
they only support eth/69 and we only advertised eth/67-68. eth/69
(EIP-7642) removes totalDifficulty from Status and adds earliestBlock/
latestBlock/latestBlockHash fields instead.

- Advertise eth/69 capability in Hello alongside eth/67-68
- Add eth/69 Status encode/decode (no TD, block range fields)
- Route to correct Status format based on negotiated version

commit.message:
Include received POs in sync, use QB ReceivedQuantity for readiness

Parser now keeps fully received POs (status='received') instead of
skipping them. Only manually closed (cancelled) POs are filtered out.
This means QB's ReceivedQuantity on PO lines has real data.

Simplified both readiness endpoints to use qty_received from PO lines
directly instead of cross-referencing item receipts (linked_po was
always empty anyway).

commit.message:
fix: send Deepgram KeepAlive during TTS playback to prevent timeout

While TTS plays the greeting, no caller audio is forwarded to Deepgram
(to avoid self-hearing). This caused Deepgram to time out (net0001)
before the caller could speak. Now sends KeepAlive messages every 200ms
during TTS playback.

Phase XXXIII: Finite Field Arithmetic & Galois Theory (T456-T470)

15 theorems, 75 tests — all passing. GF(3) through GF(81) extensions,
cyclotomic polynomials at q reproducing all SRG parameters, field
extension tower with degrees lambda and mu, irreducible polynomial
counts, trace/norm maps (norm exponent=v at GF(81)), quadratic
residue counts matching SRG values, primitive roots (phi(q^3-1)=k),
Artin-Wedderburn decomposition, Galois cohomology (Hilbert 90),
Gauss/Jacobi sums, Weil bound, and Ihara zeta function.
470 theorems total, 2560+ tests across 32 suites.

commit.message:
v1.0.2: Self-hostable AgentTalk server — three interchangeable transports

Add agenttalk_server.py — a zero-dependency local HTTPS REST API server
that users can self-host as an alternative to Email and FTP. Same API as
the public relay at relay.agentazall.ai, so agents work on both without
any code changes.

Three transport layers, one interface:
- AgentTalk: modern HTTPS REST API (agentazall server --agenttalk)
- Email: SMTP + IMAP + POP3 (agentazall server --email)
- FTP: file-based sync (agentazall server --ftp)

All three are open, self-hostable, and interchangeable. Agents don't care
which transport delivers their messages — switch by changing one config line.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Chunk long audio for ASR to avoid CUDA OOM on 75+ min videos

separately, merge timestamps. Also clear CUDA cache after job
failures to prevent corrupted allocator state on retries.

Fix marketplace image loading: use multiple IPFS gateways and proxy on client

- token-metadata.ts: pass original ipfs:// URI to buildGatewayUrls so all
  3 gateways are tried (was passing already-resolved https://ipfs.io URL,
  which only ever tried one gateway)
- marketplace-hub-shared.ts: add buildClientMediaProxyUrl to route IPFS
  image/metadata requests through /api/ipfs proxy instead of bare gateways
- marketplace-hub.tsx: use buildClientMediaProxyUrl in client-side preview
  fallback; add 8s AbortController timeout; fetch token metadata through
  IPFS proxy to avoid CORS issues on IPFS URIs

commit.message:
Fix controller transpiler: query params, private method ivars, path helpers

Four fixes to the Rails controller filter:

1. params[:key] for non-:id keys in standard actions now reads from
   context.params.key (query params) instead of function arguments
2. Instance variables set in called private methods (e.g., setup_form)
   are now included in view render props
3. Path helper calls (*_path) in controller body are collected and
   imported so they're available inside the controller IIFE
4. No-arg path helpers (e.g., people_path) use send! to emit ()

Also adds query parameter parsing to all three runtime targets
(rails_server.js, browser/rails.js, cli.mjs).

Updates ballroom people views to match showcase patterns.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

perf: move classify from propose threads to exec queue — fix rayon contention

With window=4, 4 propose threads all called classify_transactions()
concurrently, fighting for the same rayon thread pool. This inflated
classify from 25ms to 1100ms+ and starved execution (600ms → 3756ms).

Now classify runs inside the sequential exec queue right before
execution. Each stage gets the full rayon pool — zero contention.

Before: classify=1119ms + exec=3756ms = ~4875ms per 100K block
After:  classify=~25ms  + exec=~600ms = ~625ms per 100K block (8x faster)
